title: Galatella iberica
intro: Galatella iberica, also known as Iberian gallium, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Galatella iberica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Galatella iberica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health problems and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Galatella iberica is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family consists of over 23,000 species of plants, including many well-known species such as daisies, sunflowers, and asters. Galatella iberica is further classified in the genus Galatella, which is a group of small shrubby perennials native to Europe and Western Asia. The genus Galatella is classified in the tribe Inuleae, which is a group of plants characterized by their small yellow or pink flowers and hairy leaves. Galatella iberica is a specific species within the genus Galatella, known for its unique characteristics and growth habits.
